metadata.title: The Basel Problem: Lighthouses Sum to π²/6

beats.narration_text: This is Liam for Humanitarians AI.

props.topic: HUMANITARIANS AI · The Basel Problem: Lighthouses Sum to π²/6

beats.narration_text: Hej. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of the basel problem: lighthouses sum to π²/6. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of the basel problem: lighthouses sum to π²/6
output: ✓ The Basel Problem: Lighthouses Sum to π²/6

beats.narration_text: Here is why The Basel Problem: Lighthouses Sum to π²/6 belongs in your working knowledge. The systems, policies, and tools shaped by it affect decisions at your level — whether or not you understand the mechanism. Knowing the underlying logic gives you the frame; missing it means reasoning from output with no access to the inputs.

The Basel Problem: Lighthouses Sum to π²/6 is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. 1 + 1/4 + 1/9 + ⋯ = π²/6 ≈ 1.6449 — and the reason π appears is circles.. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
beats.new_visual_element: Concept card: The Basel Problem: Lighthouses Sum to π²/6

beats.narration_text: Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of The Basel Problem: Lighthouses Sum to π²/6. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: n-slider. Verify: 1 + 1/4 + 1/9 + ⋯ = π²/6 ≈ 1.6449 — and the reason π appears is circles.. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of The Basel Problem: Lighthouses Sum to π²/6. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: n-slider. Verify: 1 + 1/4 +

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: the basel problem: lighthouses sum to π²/6. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag main slider from 1 to 200. Watch the physics respond in real time.

beats.narration_text: 1 + 1/4 + 1/9 + ⋯ = π²/6 ≈ 1.6449 — and the reason π appears is circles.. Exactly as the equations predict.

One prompt. A live demonstration of the basel problem: lighthouses sum to π²/6. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this The Basel Problem: Lighthouses Sum to π²/6 simulation and add a second slider that controls the main parameter. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
